                                VERSUS
GAUTAM     RESPONDENT
O R D E R
1. The present petition has been filed under Section 25 of the
Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (for short “CPC”) by the petitioner
seeking transfer of Matrimonial Case No.127/2024, titled as "Gautam
Vs. Surena", pending before the Principal Judge, Family Court,
Baghpat, Uttar Pradesh to the Principal Judge, Family Court,
Gurugram, Haryana. 
2. Though served, none appears for the respondent. 
3. Having gone through the material available on record and also
the facts of the case, we are of the view that this Transfer
Petition deserves to be allowed. Accordingly, Matrimonial Case
No.127/2024, titled as "Gautam Vs. Surena", pending before the
Principal  Judge,  Family  Court,  Baghpat,  Uttar  Pradesh  is
transferred  to  the  Principal  Judge,  Family  Court,  Gurugram,
Haryana. 
4. Records shall be sent by the Court where proceedings are
pending to the transferee Court promptly.
1

--- Page 364 ---
5. A copy of this order be sent to the concerned courts for
compliance.
6. The Transfer Petition stands allowed in the aforesaid terms.
7. On an application made by the parties, the Court to which the
case is transferred shall permit the parties to appear through
video conference on all dates except on the dates on which their
personal presence is absolutely mandatory.
8. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.

               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No.9932/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 15-09-2025
in CRMABA No. 7260/2025 passed by the High Court of Judicature at
Allahabad]
HARENDRA                                           Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF U.P.                                  Respondent(s)
(IA No. 154828/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T. AND 
IA No. 154820/2026 - PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS/
FACTS/ANNEXURES)
 
Date : 20-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJAY KAROL
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AUGUSTINE GEORGE MASIH
For Petitioner(s) :Ms. Moulishree Pathak, Adv.
    Mr. Niraj Dubey, Adv.
    Mr. Naresh Kumar Srivastava, Adv.
    Mr. Pradum Kumar, Adv.  
    Mr. Nishesh Kumar, Adv. 
    Mr. Nishesh Sharma, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Abhishek Saket, Adv.
    Mr. Manish Kumar, Adv.
                   Dr. Vijendra Singh, AOR
      UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. We are informed that despite passing of our order dated
20.05.2026, which  is,  in  toto,  extracted  hereunder,  the
petitioner was arrested and produced before the concerned
Court, who also did not release the petitioner despite the
order having been brought to its notice:
“1. The petitioner challenges the judgment and order
1

--- Page 391 ---
dated  15-09-2025  in  CRMABA  No.  7260/2025  titled
“Harendra vs. State of U.P. ” passed by the High Court
of Judicature at Allahabad.
2. Delay condoned.
3. Issue notice, returnable on 20.07.2026.
4. Dr.  Vijendra  Singh,  Advocate-on-Record,  accepts
notice on behalf of the State of U.P.  Hence, the formal
service of notice on the respondent-State is waived.
5. Learned counsel for the petitioner to serve a copy
of the present petition to the learned counsel for the
respondent-State within a period of one week.
6. Let counter affidavit be filed within two weeks.
Rejoinder thereto, if any, be filed within one week
thereafter.
7. In the event of arrest in connection with F.I.R.
No.30/2025 dated 14.02.2025, registered at P.S. Babugarh
District-Hapur, the petitioner shall be released on bail
by the Investigating/Arresting Officer on such terms and
conditions as imposed and found to be just, fair and
reasonable. However, in the attending facts, one of the
conditions shall necessarily be that if the petitioner
possesses a Passport, the same shall be deposited before
the Competent Authority/Court, until the conclusion of
the Trial or unless directed otherwise.
8. The petitioner shall make himself available before
the Investigating Officer on 28.05.2026 at 10:00 a.m. and
on all such dates as he may be required.
9.  Needless to add, till the investigation is not
complete in all respects, the petitioner would fully
cooperate, and if the challan/charge sheet is filed, he
would maintain good conduct and not attempt to influence
any of the witnesses in any manner till the completion
of the trial.”
2 The aforesaid order was passed in the presence of the State
counsel.  To say the least, the respondents are under contempt.
3. Issue notice to the Director General of Police (DGP), who
shall file his personal affidavit furnishing an explanation as
to why the order dated 20.05.2026 was not complied with.  
2

--- Page 392 ---
4. Also the concerned Senior Superintendent of Police (SSP)
shall remain present on the next date of hearing.
5. The Secretary General of this Court shall forthwith,
through the Registrar General of the concerned Court, seek an
explanation from the concerned judicial officer, who despite
having been brought to his notice our order, failed to take
appropriate steps.
6. Let the needful be done within one week from today.
7. We direct that the petitioner shall be released on bail
forthwith on such terms and conditions as are contained in the
order dated 20.05.2026.
8. Mr.  Abhishek  Saket,learned  counsel  for  the  State
undertakes to communicate the order to the Competent Authority
for necessary compliance during the course of the day.
9. List on 28.07.2026.
(RAJNI MUKHI)                                   (ANU BHALLA)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  15-06-2026
in BA No. 2188/2026 passed by the High Court of Delhi at New Delhi]
SULEKHA                                            Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE GOVT. OF NCT OF DELHI                    Respondent(s)
IA No. 181347/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT
IA No. 181348/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
IA  No.  202753/2026-  PERMISSION  TO  FILE  ADDITIONAL
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 20-07-2026 This matter was called for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Ajay Marwah, AoR
                   Mr. Swaroop Anand Mishra, Adv.
                   Mr. Mrigank Bhardwaj, Adv.
                   Ms. Dhriti Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Rahul Kumar, Adv.
                   Ms. Shavina Singh, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s) :Mr. D.P. Singh, ASG 
    Mr. Anmol Chandan, Adv.
    Mr. Prernal Dhal, Adv.
                   Ms. Disha Thakkar, Adv.
    Mr. S. Anand, Adv.
                   Mr. S.N. Terdal, AoR
                            O R D E R
Heard learned counsel for the parties. 
2. The present petition has been filed against the impugned order
dated 15.06.2026 passed by the High Cbhai how to reach supreme
court from sarai kale khanourt of Delhi at New Delhi in BA No.

--- Page 406 ---
2188/2026, by which the prayer for extension of the period of
interim bail granted to the petitioner on the pretext of her son
needing to undergo a surgery has been refused.
3. From the material on record and also confirmed by the learned
counsel for the State, the younger son of the petitioner, who is in
his mid twenties, is under a vegetative state and the Doctors
repeatedly have extended the time for the operation, which was
supposed to be performed on him, due to medical reasons.
4. Being conscious of such fact, on purely humanitarian grounds,
we consider that the prayer for extension of interim bail is
genuine.
5. Accordingly, for the present, the interim bail stands extended
till the end of October, 2026.
6. In view of the above , the Special Leave petition stands
disposed of.
7. In the meantime, it will be open to the petitioner to move an
application seeking regular bail before the Court concerned, which
shall be considered on its own merits without being prejudiced by
these orders.
8. Pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.
(SACHIN KUMAR SRIVASTAVA)                       (ANJALI PANWAR)
    COURT MASTER (SH)                        ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

ITEM NO.49               COURT NO.14               SECTION II-A
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No(s).  8535/2025
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 13-01-2025
in CRWP No. 842/2024 passed by the High Court of Judicature at
Bombay]
RAJU SHANTARAM KOLI                                Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF MAHARASHTRA                           Respondent(s)
 
Date : 17-07-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K.V. VISWANATHAN
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARUN PALLI
For Petitioner(s) : 
                   Mr. M. Y. Deshmukh, AOR
                   Ms. Manjeet Kirpal, Adv.
                   Mr. D. Aswathaman, Adv.
                   Mr. Atharva D. Kale, Adv.                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
                   Mr. Bharat Bagla, Adv.
                   Mr. Siddharth Dharmadhikari, Adv.
                   Mr. Aaditya Aniruddha Pande, AOR
                   Mr. Shrirang B. Varma, Adv.                  
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Mr. M.Y.Deshmukh, learned counsel appearing for the
petitioner submits that he proposes to file a Special
Leave  Petition  with  an  appropriate  application  for
condonation of delay against the judgment dated 10 th June,
2016 in Criminal Appeal No.975 of 2014.
1

--- Page 426 ---
2. The  point  involved  in  the  present  petition  is
whether two life sentences can operate consecutively in
the  same  trial.   In  this  case,  the  petitioner  is
convicted for the offences under Sections 302, 376 and
342 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860 and sentenced to
undergo life on two counts with the direction that the
sentences should run consecutively.  The High Court has
maintained the said sentence and the direction to operate
consecutively.  When the petitioner approached the High
Court with a Writ Petition, the Writ Petition has been
dismissed upholding the objection of the State that the
Court cannot review its earlier judgment by virtue of a
Writ Petition.
3. Learned  counsel  appearing  for  the  petitioner
submits  that the  petitioner is  in jail  and without
prejudice to his rights and contention that the Writ
Petition is maintainable, he proposes to file a Special
Leave Petition against the order of the High Court.
4. As prayed, two weeks’ time is granted for the same.

2. The applicant- petitioner was convicted under Section 323/34,
325/34,  326/34  and  324/34  of  IPC  and  sentenced  to  7  years
imprisonment with fine of Rs. 10000/-.
3. Learned counsel for the applicant, with reference to the custody
certificate (at page 122), submits that out of the total sentence
awarded to the applicant, he has already undergone a period of 3
years, 2 months and 10 days (excluding remission).
4. Accordingly, the I.A. No. 192749/2026 is allowed and the
petitioner is exempted from surrendering till the first date of

--- Page 452 ---
listing of the petition.
5. Office to list the matter on August 3, 2026.
(KANIKA TAYAL)                            (AJEET SINGH TARIYAL)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             BRANCH OFFICER

Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (C) No(s). 14161/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 17-03-2026
in CONTC No. 2060/2025 passed by the High Court for The State of
Telangana at Hyderabad]
A.V. RANGANATH                                     Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
A SUDHAKAR REDDY                                   Respondent(s)
FOR ADMISSION 
 
Date : 17-07-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE DIPANKAR DATTA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SHEEL NAGU
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Mukul Rohatgi, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. T. Rajinikanth Reddy, A.A.G.
                   Mr. Kumar Vaibhaw, Adv.
                   Ms. Devina Sehgal, AOR
                   Mr. Yatharth Kansal, Adv.
                   Mr. Rahul Reddy, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Syed Ahmed Saud, Adv.
                   Mr. P. Vishweswara Nikhil, Adv.
                   Mr. Daanish Ahmed Syed, Adv.
                   Mr. Mohd. Parvez Dabas, Adv.
                   Mr. Uzmi Jameel Husain, Adv.
                   Mr. Mohd Shahib, Adv.
                   Mr. Mohammad Aadil Khan, Adv.
                   M/s Shakil Ahmad Syed, AOR
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Pursuant to our earlier order dated 27th April, 2026, the petitioner has filed
a compliance affidavit.
2. It is stated in such compliance affidavit that he has substantially complied
with the High Court’s order by removing the children’s play equipment, the
open-air gym equipment, the inauguration plaque/stone and the information

--- Page 522 ---
2
board. However, justification as called for by this Court has been offered for the
purpose of retention of the gate, the fencing and the retaining wall.
3. At this stage, we do not wish to examine whether the justification is valid
and proper or not. In our considered opinion, interest of justice would be best
served if the High Court is requested to examine the justification and to take a
further decision as to whether such gate, fencing and retaining wall should be
permitted to be retained or not. We order accordingly.
4. It shall be open to the respondent to put in his objection to the compliance
report, filed in this Court, before the High Court within two weeks.
5. The  finding  that  the  petitioner  is  guilty  of  contempt  shall  remain
suspended till such time the High Court decides the issue as per this order.
Should the High Court find the justification not to be valid and proper, the
suspension ordered hereby shall stand lifted and it may pass appropriate orders
as the occasion demands. 
6. The High Court is requested to decide the issue in accordance with law as
early as possible. The main matter, i.e., Civil Miscellaneous Appeal No. 175 of
2025 may also be decided expeditiously.
7. The special leave petition is disposed of on the above terms.
8. Pending interlocutory application(s), if any, shall also stand disposed of.
(JATINDER KAUR)                             (SUDHIR KUMAR SHARMA)
P.S. to REGISTRAR                             COURT MASTER (NSH)

1. Hearing the grievance of the petitioner that the writ petition instituted by
him before the High Court of Judicature at Allahabad in 2014 had not been listed
post 24th November, 2017, we had called for a report from the Registrar General
of the High Court by an order dated 28 th April, 2026. The Registrar General was
required to delineate in the report the various dates on which the petitioner’s
writ petition was listed before learned Judges having assignment to hear the
same.
2. A report dated 27 th May, 2026 of the Registrar General of the High Court

--- Page 524 ---
2
has been placed on record. We have perused the report. It appears that on
several dates post 24 th November, 2017, the writ petition was listed but could
not be taken up for diverse reasons.
3. Petitioner,  therefore,  is  not  quite  right  in  his  claim  that  post  24th
November, 2017, the writ petition was not listed.
4. Be that as it may, we dispose of the special leave petition without making
any order as prayed by the petitioner. 
5. Petitioner may, however, make a request to the roster Bench for hearing
the writ petition, which is more than a decade old, at an early date.
6. Pending interlocutory application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
(JATINDER KAUR)                             (SUDHIR KUMAR SHARMA)
P.S. to REGISTRAR                             COURT MASTER (NSH)

--- Page 525 ---

--- Page 632 ---
    “1. Delay condoned.

pleaded no instructions when the matter was called

1

--- Page 640 ---
SLP (C) No. 10147 of 2026

number.

when the matter is called out again in the pass over
round, learned counsel for the petitioner is not
present.

option left with this Court except to dismiss the
Special  Leave  Petition  for  want  of  prosecution.

Mr. Nishe Rajen Shonker, AOR
                   
      UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Learned counsel for the appellant submits that
the appellant has already undergone approximately 10
years including one year remission in custody.
2. Considering  the  period  undergone  by  the
appellant and the fact that the appeal is likely to

--- Page 658 ---
2
take  some  time,  we  are  inclined  to  suspend  the
sentence and enlarge the appellant on bail pending
disposal of the appeal by this Court.
3. Accordingly, pending disposal of the appeal, we
suspend the sentence of the appellant and enlarge the
appellant on bail subject to the satisfaction of the
concerned Trial Court in connection with Sessions Case
No.1009/2015 for the offence punishable under Sections
302 & 307 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860.
4. The prayer for bail is disposed of in the above
terms.
(NIRMALA NEGI)                               (MANOJ KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                    COURT MASTER (NSH)

We  have  heard  the  learned  Senior  counsel
appearing for the petitioner and the learned ASG
appearing for the respondent.
In as much as the matter is fixed for further
hearing on 8th August,2026 before the Trial Court, we
are not inclined to pass any order in this petition.

--- Page 672 ---
2
However, a direction is issued to the Trial
Court to expedite the trial and conclude the same
within a period of six months from the date of
receipt of a copy of this order.
In case the trial is not completed within a
period of six months, liberty is granted to the
petitioner to file a fresh bail application before
the Trial Court and in that case the earlier order
passed which has been confirmed, shall not stand in
the way of the petitioner.
The Special Leave Petition stands disposed of,
accordingly.
Pending application(s), if any, shall also stand
disposed of.

ITEM NO.7               COURT NO.14               SECTION II-D
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
SPECIAL LEAVE PETITION (CRIMINAL) Diary No(s). 31989/2026
[Arising out of impugned judgment and order dated  01-09-2025 in BA
No. 1954/2025 passed by the High Court of Delhi at New Delhi]
NEELAM                                             Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
RUBY & ANR.                                        Respondent(s)
IA No. 191036/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN FILING, IA No.
191034/2026 - PERMISSION TO FILE PETITION (SLP/TP/WP/..)
 
Date : 15-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K.V. VISWANATHAN
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARUN PALLI
For Petitioner(s)  Ms. Rashmi Nandakumar, AOR
                   Ms. Yashmita Pandey, Adv.                   
                   
For Respondent(s) Mr. Brijender Chahar, ASG
Mr. Abhinav Singh, Adv.
Mr. Karan Chahar, Adv.
Mr. Kartik K.Sharma, Adv.
Mr. S.N.Terdal, AOR
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Delay condoned.
2. Heard Mr. Rashmi Nandakumar, learned counsel for the
petitioner  and  Mr.  Brijender  Chahar,  learned  Additional
Solicitor General appearing for the State.
3. Having perused the impugned order, we are not inclined to
interfere with the same.
4. At this stage, learned counsel for the petitioner submits
that if the respondent no.1 is enlarged on bail, claim of
parity will arise.

--- Page 678 ---
2
5. That apprehension is not justified for the reason that
the respondent no.1 has been enlarged on bail considering the
fact that she is a lady and more particularly, considering her
medical condition. This  factum is not even disputed by the
learned counsel for the petitioner. Further, respondent no.1
has already undergone about one year and six months as an
under  trial.  In  view  of  all  these  peculiarities,  the
respondent no.1’s case stands on a separate footing.
6. With the above observations, the Special Leave Petition
is dismissed.
7. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
(NIRMALA NEGI)                                  (MANOJ KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

                             O R D E R
1. Ms.  Harshita  Nathrani,  learned  counsel  for  the
petitioner, on being asked where the petitioner Ruchi Dabur is
residing, submits that the petitioner is residing in Rohini,
Delhi and is not residing in Ghaziabad, Uttar Pradesh.
2. Learned counsel for the petitioner prays for a week’s
time to file an affidavit in this regard.

4. The affidavit will clarify as to:-
1) where the petitioner is residing;
2) the address of the premises where she is residing;

--- Page 682 ---
2
3) how long the premises has been in occupation of the
petitioner;
4) whether the petitioner has a premises in Ghaziabad,
Uttar Pradesh;
5) If the petitioner’s case is that she is residing in
Rohini Delhi, since when she has been residing in
Rohini, Delhi;
6) If the petitioner is in a rented accommodation in
Rohini, it will also be set out as to when the
premises was taken on rent and since when rent is
being paid to the land-lord.

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Mr.  Ambhoj  Kumar  Sinha,  Advocate  has  caused
appearance on behalf of the Respondent No. 1 (Husband).
2. At the outset, learned counsel for the parties submit
that the matter be referred for mediation to explore the
possibility of a settlement and to provide a quietus to
all pending disputes inter se the parties.
3. Accordingly, the matter is referred to the Supreme
Court  Mediation  Centre.  The  parties  are  requested  to
appear in-person or through video conferencing before the
Mediator nominated by the Co-ordinator of the Supreme

1

--- Page 814 ---
clear that in the event of any of the conditions imposed for grant
of bail is violated, the petitioner would be at liberty to move
the jurisdictional trial court for cancellation of the bail and in
the event of such an application being filed, the jurisdictional
court shall consider the same on its own merits necessarily after
affording respondent No.1 – accused an opportunity. 
3. All pending applications stand consigned to records.
SLP (Crl.) No(s).12631/2026:
Learned Senior Counsel appearing for the petitioner seeks
leave of the Court to withdraw the petition and pursue his
grievance before the High Court which is adjudicating the matter.
Hence, without expressing any opinion on the merits of the case,
we permit the petitioner to withdraw this petition. We request the
High Court to expedite the hearing subject to cooperation of the
learned counsels. 
Hence, Special Leave Petition is dismissed as withdrawn. 
All contentions of both parties are kept open.

ITEM NO.44               COURT NO.10               SECTION IX-A
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (C)  No(s).1450/2025
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 26-08-2022
in WP No.1291/2020 passed by the High Court of Judicature at Bombay
at Nagpur]
NARESH MAHADEO BORKAR                              Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF MAHARASHTRA & ORS.                    Respondent(s)
 
Date : 17-07-2026 This petition was called for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s)  Mr. Dushyant Parashar, AoR
                   Mr. Manu Parashar, Adv.
                   Mr. Dinesh Pandey, Adv.
                   Mr. Amit Pratap Shaunak, Adv.
                   Mr. Praduman Kumar Agarwal, Adv.
                   Mr. Sanjay Agarwal, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s)  Mr. Samrat Krishnarao Shinde, Adv.
                   Mr. Siddharth Dharmadhikari, Adv.
                   Mr. Aaditya Aniruddha Pande, AoR
                   Mr. Shrirang B. Varma, Adv.                   
                   
                   Mr. Suhaskumar Kadam, Adv.
         For M/S. Black & White Solicitors, AoR          
                   
          
                             O R D E R
Heard learned counsels for the parties.
2. The  petitioner  has  assailed  the  impugned  order  dated
26.08.2022 passed by the High Court of Judicature at Bombay, Nagpur
Bench in W.P. No.1291 of 2020 by which, his challenge to his
non-continuation/termination of employment as Encroachment Removal

--- Page 826 ---
2
Officer  on  contractual  basis  vide  order  of  Akola  Municipal
Corporation dated 27.06.2019 has not been interfered with.
3. Learned counsels for the parties have addressed the Court,
both on facts as well as on law.
4. However,  taking  a  holistic  view  and  in  the  facts  and
circumstances  of  the  present  case,  we  are  not  persuaded  to
interfere in the impugned order.
5. Accordingly, the present petition stands disposed of.
6. At this juncture, learned counsel for the petitioner submitted
that the petitioner is about forty-six years old and still has
scope of seeking employment elsewhere as he has a family to support
and thus, the Court may indicate that whatever orders have been
passed in the present proceedings, may not prejudicially affect the
petitioner  with  regard  to  his  consideration  for  any  future
employment before any institution or forum.
7. Having regard to the aforesaid, we only indicate that if at
all, the petitioner participates in any exercise for his employment
or otherwise, any order passed in the present proceedings before
this Court, High Court or previous proceedings before the Labour or
Industrial Court shall not prejudice him from being considered for
the post for which he may apply, which shall be considered by the
authorities concerned, in accordance with law.
8. Pending application(s), if any, shall also stand disposed of.
(SAPNA BISHT)                                   (ANJALI PANWAR)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             ASSISTANT  REGISTRAR

                             O R D E R
It is submitted by the learned counsel appearing
for the petitioner that despite best efforts, the
petitioner could not succeed in getting the Special
Appeal taken up before the Uttarakhand High Court.
1

--- Page 928 ---
It is further submitted that tomorrow, i.e.
21.07.2026, is the last date for counselling. As such,
an interim protection may be granted to the petitioner
without creating any equities so that in the event the
petitioner, ultimately, succeeds, he may not suffer.
Application seeking impleadment is allowed.
Issue notice, returnable on 27.07.2026.
Dasti notice, in addition, is permitted.
The petitioner to take necessary steps to serve
the respondents forthwith and file proof of service by
the next date.
Provisionally, the petitioner is permitted to
participate in the JoSAA 2026 counselling, scheduled
for 21.07.2026.
It is made clear that in case the petitioner
gets an admission under the said counselling in the
specified category of General-Person with Benchmark
Disability  (General-PwBD),  it  will  not  create  any
equity and the same shall remain subject to the final
outcome of these petitions.

1. Upon being mentioned, the matter is taken on Board.

carefully perusing the material placed on record, we do not find
any ground to entertain the petition filed under Article 32 of the
Constitution.

to the petitioner to approach the jurisdictional High Court.

before us by the petitioner are permitted to be raised before the
High Court.

the merits of the case.

disposed of.

ITEM NO.85               COURT NO.1               SECTION X
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Writ Petition(s)(Criminal)  No(s).  265/2026
PANKAJ SHARMA                                      Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
GOVERNMENT OF NCT OF DELHI & ORS.                  Respondent(s)
(IA No. 199384/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.)
 
Date : 16-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE THE CHIEF JUSTICE
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE JOYMALYA BAGCHI
         HON'BLE MRS. JUSTICE V. MOHANA
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. Vikas Singh, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Sidharth Dave, Sr. Adv.
                   Ms. Deepika Kalia, Adv.
                   Mr. Sudeep Chandra, Adv.
                   Ms. Khushi, Adv.
                   Mr. Rahul Kaushik, Adv.
                   Mr. Ajit Prasad, Adv.
                   Mr. Yatinder Chaudhary, Adv.
Mr. Manish Dubey, Adv.
Ms. Pragya Baghel, Adv.
Mr. Rajeev Dubey, Adv.
Mr. Yatinder Chaudhary, Adv.
Mr. Shekhar Raj Sharma, Adv.
Mr. Vikas Malhotra, Adv.
Mr. Durgesh Kumar Mishra, Adv.
Mr. Himanshu Tyagi, Adv.
Ms. Dipika Kalia, Adv.
                   Mr. Tarun Gupta, AOR
Mr. T.A. Khan, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) : Mr. R. Venkataramani, AG
Mr. Chitvan Singhal, Adv.
Mr. Kartikay Agarwal, Adv.
Mr. Adarsh Kumar Pandey, Adv. 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. We have heard Mr. Vikas Singh and other senior counsel on
behalf of the petitioner-victim. Learned Attorney General for India
1

--- Page 958 ---
has assisted us on behalf of the investigating agency. The record
has also been perused.  
2. Taking into consideration the nature of injury to the vital
part(s) of the body, it seems to us that, prima facie, offences
like attempt to murder ought to have been incorporated in the FIR,
subject to the final outcome of the investigation. Consequently, we
are of the view that the FIR ought to have also been lodged under
Sections 109 and 118 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023.
3. Since allegations of pressurising the petitioner to amicably
settle the dispute have been attributed to the local police, in the
peculiar facts and circumstances of the case, we direct the
Commissioner of Police, Delhi to transfer the investigation to the
Crime Branch of the Delhi Police.
4. The direction contained in paragraph 5 of our order dated
14.07.2026 shall continue to operate till the investigation is
complete. The transferee investigating agency will conduct the
investigation independently, fairly, and dispassionately. If it is
found during the course of the investigation that any other prima
facie offence is made out, the appropriate section will be added to
the FIR.
5. With these directions, the Writ Petition stands disposed of
with pending application(s), if any. 
(NITIN TALREJA)                            (PREETHI DILEEP KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        DY. REGISTRAR
2

--- Page 959 ---
REVISED

Date : 20-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.B. PARDIWALA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Makarand D. Adkar, Adv.
                   Mr. Rishabh Jain, Adv.
                   Mr. Rajiv Shankar Dvivedi, AOR               
                   
For Respondent(s) :Ms. Pritha Srikumar Iyer, AOR
                   Ms. Mansa Shukla, Adv.
                   Ms. Akhila Balaji, Adv.
                   Ms. Mrinalini Dave, Adv.
                   
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Having  heard  the  learned  counsel  appearing  for  the
petitioners and having gone through the materials on record, we
find no good ground to interfere with the impugned order passed

                             O R D E R
1. The delay of 1517 days having not been explained with cogent
reasons so as to constitute sufficient cause as prescribed under
Section 5 of the Limitation Act, 1963, we are not inclined to
condone this inordinate delay. Hence, we also do not propose to
examine the prayer of the petitioner(s) on merits. Hence, Special
Leave Petitions stand dismissed on the ground of delay.

                           [IN CHAMBER]
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. Mukesh Kumar, AOR

For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. IA No. 196416/2026 is an application for grant of exemption from
surrendering.  In  the  present  case,  the  petitioner  has  been
sentenced to undergo three years R.I. for his conviction for an
offence under Section 304, Part-II, I.P.C., 1860.  He has stated
that the petitioner was on bail during the pendency of the appeal
as well as during the trial.
2. After hearing learned counsel for the petitioner, this Court is
of the opinion that the application for grant of exemption from
surrendering deserves to be allowed and is accordingly allowed.  
3. List the matter after four weeks.

PHOTOQUIP INDIA LTD                                Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
A.K. NAIKWADI . & ANR.                             Respondent(s)
FOR SUITOR FUND AMOUNT 
 
Date : 16-07-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SATISH CHANDRA SHARMA
                           [IN CHAMBER]
For Petitioner(s) : By Courts Motion, AOR
                    Mr. Vikash Singh, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The office report reflects that the appellant had deposited an
amount of Rs. 50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) at the time of
filing of the appeal and the same is treated as suitor’s fund
amount.  Till date, no application is filed on behalf of the
parties for release of suitor’s fund amount.
2. Considering the above referred facts, this Court is of the
opinion that this Court can exercise its discretion under Rule 7,
Order XXIV of the Supreme Court Rules, 2013 by allowing the
disbursement of the suitor’s fund amount of Rs. 50,000/- (Rupees
fifty thousand) in favour of the Armed Forces Battle Casualties
Welfare Fund (AFBCWF).
3. Registry to take necessary steps within three weeks.
4.  Miscellaneous  application,  if  any,  stands  disposed  of
accordingly.
(KANIKA TAYAL)                                    (KOMAL)
COURT MASTER (SH)                               COURT MASTER (NSH)
